findings:
Today's tape concentrates **eleven 5/5 matches** — the densest convergence the validation pipeline has produced this month — across three structural axes that all close in the same 72-hour window: AAPL Q2 FY26 closing the Mag 7 super-week with a $111.2B revenue print + $100B buyback + 4% dividend hike, the Apr 27 Microsoft–OpenAI partnership reset operationalized through the Apr 28 OpenAI-on-Bedrock launch (the first cross-cloud frontier-model deployment in the post-exclusivity world), and the CSAI Foundation MITRE-CNA authorization landing as the first agentic-AI-specific CVE issuer.

The **Apr 27 / Apr 28 / Apr 29 / Apr 30 Mag-7-capex-disclosure prediction quartet** lands in unison: AAPL's Services record $30.98B (+16%) without a separate AI-services breakout is the disclosure-gap the predictions identified; Big Tech 2026 AI capex tracking $700B with Q1 capex >$130B (Alphabet+Meta+Microsoft+Amazon) is the buyside-pressure number; the Apr 29 MSFT $37B AI run-rate single-line disclosure remains the operational precedent. Apr 30 close S&P 500 7,196.01 (+1.02%, first-ever above 7,200) and April being the best month since 2020 (Alphabet +34% on the month) shows the tape is rallying through the disclosure pressure rather than capitulating to it — but the disclosure rewrite is still materializing because the next print cycle (Q2 FY26) lands directly into the next FOMC pair (June 17 + July 29).

The **Apr 25 / Apr 26 / Apr 27 / Apr 29 / Apr 30 AI-security-thesis line** lands its single largest structural validation in the validation pipeline's history. **CSAI Foundation Apr 29 announced as MITRE-authorized CVE Numbering Authority specifically for agentic-AI vulnerabilities — the first agentic-AI-specific CVE issuer in MITRE's program** — is the institutional-landing event the Apr 27 "AI-Infra CVE class graduates into regulatory primitive" forecast called for, arriving ahead of the Q4 schedule. Combined with the STAR for AI Catastrophic Risk Annex (4-phase rollout June 2026 → Dec 2027 aligned with NIST AI RMF / EU AI Act / ISO/IEC 42001), AARM (Vanta), and ATF (transferred from MassiveScale.AI) specs, this is the structural infrastructure for the predicted regulatory regime. **MITRE ATT&CK v19 (Apr 28)** carry layers the named-technique catalogue (context poisoning, tool invocation abuse, MCP server compromise, malicious agent deployment) on top.

The **inference-server CVE thesis** (Apr 26 / Apr 28) lifts to 5/5 on a multi-CVE concentration: **LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 (CVSS 9.3) — pre-auth SQLi against the Authorization header on /chat/completions; one row leaks OpenAI org keys + Anthropic admin tokens + AWS Bedrock IAM credentials simultaneously; first observed exploitation 26h post-advisory; attackers automated origin-IP rotation 21 minutes apart** — is the canonical 2026 LLM-gateway-as-blast-radius case. **SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) remains unpatched ~12 days post-disclosure into May 1**, the longest-running 2026 unpatched LLM-tooling CVE. **CISA KEV Apr 30 adds CVE-2026-41940 cPanel & WHM** with a federal deadline May 21 — the largest single-product KEV addition in the cycle. The **Apr 30 fresh CVE wave** (CVE-2026-7503 / 6389 / 6543 / 7551, all CVSS 8.8) plus **Spring AI CVE-2026-40967** carry extends the model-tooling-layer-injection class to LiteLLM gateway + Spring AI RAG + SGLang inference + vLLM serving + LMDeploy serving simultaneously — top-to-bottom AI-stack supply chain.

The **Apr 25 MCP-attack-surface-bottleneck → MCP Gateway / Firewall product wave** thesis lands at 5/5: **OpenAI Agents SDK Apr 30 ships native sandbox execution as default primitive across 7 sandbox providers (Blaxel/Cloudflare/Daytona/E2B/Modal/Runloop/Vercel)**, baking the MCP-Gateway-class commercial mitigation pattern into the foundation-lab reference SDK. **Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 GA + Foundry Toolkit for VS Code GA + Toolbox preview + hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service preview + Observability in Foundry Control Plane GA (Apr 30)** is the parallel Microsoft-side substrate. The Apr 23 "Agent Control Plane" thematic carries on the same evidence.

The **Apr 25 / Apr 26 hyperscaler-AI-lab capital-coupling → multi-frontier-vendor enterprise strategy** prediction lands at 5/5: **Apr 27 Microsoft–OpenAI reset (non-exclusive license through 2032; revenue-share asymmetry; AWS commitments fungible with OpenAI usage) + Apr 28 OpenAI-on-Bedrock (limited preview, GPT-5.5 + Codex + Bedrock Managed Agents)** is OpenAI's first non-Azure home for first-party model serving and the structural counter to the Apr 28 WSJ revenue-miss tape. The "model-portability promise of 2024-2025 quietly ends" is no longer forecast; it has happened.

The **Apr 30 Powell-stays-on-Board prediction** lands at 5/5 inside one day: Powell confirms staying on the Fed Board past May 15 Chair-term expiration (first since 1948); rejects "shadow chair" framing; Warsh nomination confirmed by Senate Banking same day; full-Senate vote pending before May 15; **Apr Jobs Report releases May 1 8:30 AM EDT (forecast 135K vs 178K Mar)** — the first non-earnings macro tape into the May 15 Chair handoff window. Markets are now pricing forward-guidance volatility on rates separately from institutional-risk on the Fed itself, exactly as forecast.

Three predictions sit at low relevance today: Apr 24's "Physical-AI 8h production runs" at 2 (Tesla / Figure / Boston Dynamics carry-forward without fresh production-record release; SoftBank Roze AI Apr 30 is a fresh data-center-construction physical-AI vector but does not yet ship an 8h-continuous-operation procurement RFP) and Apr 25's "IROS / GTC league tables" at 2 (no fresh production-record publication today). Apr 24's "capital markets reset valuation of AI-agent-displaced enterprise SaaS" sits at 3: the Mag 7 monthly rally rather than the predicted SaaS-side selloff is the dominant tape, with the chip / foundation-lab side continuing to absorb the disclosure pressure. Apr 27's "US state ships Statutory Identity for Autonomous Agents Act" sits at 3 because the federal/agency rails (CSAI MITRE-CNA, NIST CAISI RFI, Microsoft Zero Trust for AI) are setting before any state legislative move.

The dormant entry **20260420-3 "Headless Everything"** is not yet due for periodic ping (next 2026-05-11) but Layer 1 produced one mechanical keyword hit ("Simon Willison" in Other Standout Topics). The hit was added to the validation table with the `[REVIVED]` marker per protocol, but honest relevance is 1: Willison's Apr 30 entries are RSS-for-vibe-coded-apps, the Codex CLI 0.128.0 changelog, and the Zig anti-AI contribution policy — none about Headless Everything. Layer 2 semantic scan produced no match.
relation_to_my_preds:
**On "malicious local LLMs functioning like malware + zero-trust as the fundamental safeguard":** Today's tape is the structural-confirmation event your thesis has been building toward. **CSAI Foundation Apr 29 — Cloud Security Alliance authorized as MITRE CNA for agentic-AI vulnerabilities — is the first agentic-AI-specific CVE issuer**, the institutional regime your "intentionally designed system paths AI alone cannot access" framing demands. STAR for AI Catastrophic Risk Annex (4-phase rollout June 2026 → Dec 2027 aligned with NIST AI RMF / EU AI Act / ISO/IEC 42001) is the multi-jurisdiction ratification scaffolding; AARM (Autonomous Action Runtime Management, contributed by Vanta) is the runtime-enforcement layer; ATF (Agentic Trust Framework, transferred from MassiveScale.AI) is the policy spec. **MITRE ATT&CK v19 (Apr 28)** continues as the named-technique catalogue (context poisoning, tool invocation abuse, MCP server compromise, malicious agent deployment) — exactly the "internally driven multifunctional behaviors that may trigger breaches or data leakage" framing you described, now codified. **OpenAI Agents SDK Apr 30 ships native sandbox execution as a default primitive across seven sandbox providers**, hardening the harness; **Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 GA + Foundry Agent Service hosted-agents preview + Foundry Control Plane Observability GA (Apr 30)** is the Microsoft-side substrate; **AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime + Managed Harness (carry)** completes the three-cloud agent-runtime hardening triangle. The **LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208** breach is concrete evidence that the model-gateway / authorization layer is the single highest-blast-radius primitive on the agentic stack — and it is exactly the layer your zero-trust framing instructs us to design AI out of: one row in the credentials table leaks OpenAI org keys + Anthropic admin tokens + AWS Bedrock IAM credentials simultaneously. The behaviour-monitoring half of your prediction — "solutions that claim to monitor and manage behaviour will likely attract attention in only the short term" — continues to track: the monitoring stack (arxiv 2604.25562 SnapGuard, arxiv 2604.01194 AgentWatcher, arxiv 2604.15368 LogJack, Google + Forcepoint indirect-prompt-injection in-the-wild reports) is densifying but is not a substitute for the zero-trust path-design at the credential / data-access tier.

**On "cloud APIs for advanced tasks + local LLMs for daily operations, driven by SaaS price rises":** Today's tape produces both the demand-side structural event (cross-cloud frontier-model availability now operational) and the supply-side densification you predicted. On the demand side, **Apr 27 Microsoft–OpenAI partnership reset + Apr 28 OpenAI-on-Bedrock (limited preview, GPT-5.5 + Codex + Bedrock Managed Agents) + Apr 30 Codex CLI 0.128.0 model-provider-owned discovery (AWS Bedrock account state exposed to clients)** mean enterprise procurement now picks "model + cloud + SLA" as a tuple rather than "model" alone; this is the structural lever that translates electricity-cost / capacity-cost pressure into per-customer pricing. **Big Tech 2026 AI capex tracking $700B + Alphabet+Meta+Microsoft+Amazon Q1 capex >$130B + AAPL +$100B buyback authorization** is the cohort signal that capex spend is being underwritten by capital-return capacity — which, as you predicted, makes "service delivery models and pricing structures" the natural next disclosure surface. The **WSJ Apr 28 OpenAI-revenue-miss + Apr 29 META -7.5% on capex shortfall + OpenAI's response: $8/month plan + 122M paid-subscriber projection by 2026 + $100M-annualized ad pilot (projection $2.4-$2.5B 2026)** are the first concrete pricing-architecture moves directly attributable to the capacity-cost / buyside-pressure axis you identified. On the supply side, **Foundry Local GA carry (Apr 9, ~20MB native, WinML execution providers, Foundry Catalog hardware-matched model auto-download for GPT-OSS / Qwen / DeepSeek / Mistral / Phi / Whisper, OpenAI Responses API)** is the missing Windows-default integration point; **Bonsai-8B (44 tok/s iPhone 17 Pro Max / 131 tok/s M4 Pro / 368 tok/s RTX 4090; 1.15 GB total file)** + **Unsloth 2026.4.8 (inference 20-30% faster + Gemma-4-E2B 8GB VRAM training)** + **DeepSeek V4-Flash $0.14/$0.28 per M tokens** + **Qwen3.6 + Kimi K2.6 + GLM-5.1** + **TurboQuant + SGLang #21419/#23134 + PolyKV + NVIDIA kvpress + arxiv 2604.04722 Don't Waste Bits!** keep building local-side capability and per-token-cost suppression. The bifurcation infrastructure is converging on schedule; the consumer-facing AI-SaaS price hike has not landed today as an explicit headline, but the disclosure pressure (MSFT $37B AI run-rate Apr 29) is the leading indicator and the cross-cloud frontier-model availability is the operational pivot.

**On "RL/LLM-based forecasting performance improvement becomes widespread":** Today's report carries the strongest substrate-densification signal yet. The agent-skills / harness / protocol layer is now codified as a regulatory and named-technique surface (CSAI MITRE-CNA, MITRE ATT&CK v19, NIST CAISI RFI, Microsoft Zero Trust for AI), giving an RL-trained forecaster a stable target to optimize against. **OpenAI Agents SDK Apr 30 — model-native harness, native sandbox execution, sandbox-aware orchestration, configurable memory, Codex-like filesystem tools, with Blaxel / Cloudflare / Daytona / E2B / Modal / Runloop / Vercel as first-class sandbox providers** — is the foundation-lab reference harness that any RL-on-the-harness research project would build against; **Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 + Foundry Toolbox preview + hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service preview + Foundry Control Plane Observability GA** is the Microsoft-side parallel; **AWS Bedrock AgentCore Runtime Node.js + Managed Harness (carry) + AgentCore Skills on Kiro Power + Claude Cowork in Bedrock (carry)** complete the three-cloud agent-runtime substrate. **arxiv 2604.25562 SnapGuard (lightweight prompt-injection detection for screenshot-based web agents)** + **arxiv 2604.01194 AgentWatcher (rule-based prompt-injection monitor with output-attribution to causally-influential context segments)** + **arxiv 2604.15368 LogJack (cloud-log indirect-prompt-injection against LLM debugging agents; command-execution rates 0.0% to 86.2% across model classes)** + carry **arxiv 2604.24026 SSL Representation for Agent Skills** + carry **arxiv 2604.24971 PolyKV** describe the evaluation surfaces an RL forecaster would optimize against. **Google DeepMind "Vision Banana" Apr 30 — single AI system outperforming five specialized models across vision tasks; zero-shot transfer claimed; positioned for robotics + autonomous-vehicle simplification** is a direct universal-foundation precedent for the same generalization pattern the RL-trained forecaster pattern applies. No published RL-trained-forecaster paper on real-world forecasting landed today, but the harness substrate is now densifying on a single design surface across all three clouds plus two foundation-lab reference SDKs — which is the precondition the prediction implies.
